chunk_summary: Guidance on dietary fat in childhood emphasizes energy needs, fat quality, and DHA/ARA counseling, with specific recommendations for healthy children, preterm infants, and Indian dietary practices.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of dietary fat in energy density, vitamin absorption, and neurodevelopment in children.
Learn fat intake guidelines for toddlers, older children, and adolescents, including Indian-specific practices.
Recognize the importance of DHA and ARA for preterm infants and healthy dietary sources for term infants and children.
key_facts: Fat should supply 30–40% of energy in toddlers and 25–35% in older children and adolescents.
Saturated fat should be kept below 10% of total energy after 2 years, and industrial trans-fat should be eliminated.
Preterm infants require DHA at 30–65 mg/kg/day with an ARA:DHA ratio of 0.5–2.
Energy needs for preterm infants are approximately 115–140 kcal/kg/day, with protein intake of 3.5–4.0 g/kg/day.
Indian dietary counseling includes rotating household oils (mustard, groundnut, soybean, sesame) and avoiding vanaspati and reheated oils.
reference_quote: The FSSAI limit on industrial trans-fat supports this counseling.
document_key_topics: Dietary fat intake guidelines for children
DHA and ARA requirements for preterm infants
